summary: Lesions in endocarditis associated with rheumatic heart disease occur primarily along the lines of valve closure due to cumulative mechanical stress and pre-existing scarring from chronic inflammation. This specific anatomic pattern results from biomechanical vulnerabilities created by recurrent rheumatic damage, leading to targeted infection foci within already compromised valve structures.
